Matter improperly contested by Plaintiff's counsel. All parties are present. Court allows argument.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF YASMIN ABDULMALIK'S Motion To Amend Operative Complaint (C.C.P 473, 576)

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file an amended complaint is denied as to any allegations supporting claims for sex and pregnancy based harassment and granted in all other respects. Plaintiff may file her proposed first amended complaint either deleting her claims for sex and pregnancy based harassment or stating that those claims have been eliminated from the case as a result of the order granting Cruise's motion for judgment on the pleading as to those claims. None of the defendants appear to have any substantive objections to plaintiff alleging additional matters as to her claims other than sex and pregnancy based harassment and California's strong policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings favors allowing her to do so. Plaintiff must file her first amended complaint by March 5, 2025.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in complaince with CRC 3.1312.
